status_messages.what_was_done: Collected intent: target='customers', purpose='get the customer segmenation based on their spending behavio'
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not validate that the dataset file actually exists.

user_intent.business_purpose: get the customer segmenation based on their spending behaviour in transactions

status_messages.what_was_done: Profiled 50,000×23 dataset. Found 11 numeric cols. Claude suggested 6 feature groups.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not sample sub-populations or run hypothesis tests for distribution differences.
status_messages.doubts: Suggested groups are based on column names; actual feature buildability depends on data quality. Mean skewness=3.6.
issues: Mean feature skewness=3.6 is high. Log-transform recommended before clustering.

transaction_amount_behaviour.description: Captures how much a customer spends per transaction, including central tendency, spread, and extremes of their spend distribution
transaction_amount_behaviour.rationale: Spend amount is the most direct signal of spending behaviour. Aggregating per customer yields mean spend, median spend, std deviation, max single transaction, and total lifetime spend — core dimensions for segmenting low/mid/high-value customers

spending_category_profile.description: Captures the distribution of a customer's spending across merchant categories, revealing lifestyle and preference patterns (e.g. grocery-heavy vs travel-heavy vs entertainment-focused)
spending_category_profile.rationale: Category mix differentiates customer archetypes beyond raw spend level. Features include share of spend per category, dominant category, category diversity (entropy), and number of distinct categories used

transaction_frequency_timing.description: Captures how often and when a customer transacts, including recency, frequency, and temporal patterns such as weekend vs weekday or day vs night activity
transaction_frequency_timing.rationale: Frequency and recency are foundational RFM dimensions. Time-of-day and day-of-week patterns reveal behavioural rhythms (e.g. night-time spenders, weekend shoppers) that are highly segmentation-relevant

geographic_mobility.description: Captures whether a customer shops locally or across a wide geographic spread, by measuring the distance between their home location and merchant locations
geographic_mobility.rationale: Distance between customer and merchant signals mobility and urban vs rural context. Customers in dense urban areas may transact very close to home; high-mobility customers may indicate business travellers or online-heavy shoppers. Mean and max transaction distance per customer are key derived features

fraud_risk_profile.description: Captures the proportion and count of fraudulent transactions associated with a customer or their account, reflecting account vulnerability or anomalous usage patterns
fraud_risk_profile.rationale: Even if fraud labels are not a target, customers with higher fraud rates may represent a distinct behavioural segment (compromised accounts, risky merchants, unusual patterns). Features include fraud rate per customer, total fraudulent amount, and fraud transaction count

customer_demographics.description: Captures stable customer attributes — age derived from date of birth and gender — that correlate with spending behaviour and help interpret cluster personas
customer_demographics.rationale: Age and gender are classic demographic segmentation dimensions. Age in particular correlates strongly with spending category preferences and amounts. These features add interpretability to clusters and help name personas meaningfully

status_messages.what_was_done: Engineered 290 features for 983 customers from 1,296,675 transactions. Builders used: ['category_behavior', 'category_trend', 'category_loyalty', 'overall_spend', 'overall_frequency', 'merchant_diversity', 'geographic_mobility', 'temporal_patterns', 'demographic'].
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not engineer features requiring external data (e.g. credit scores, product catalogue).
status_messages.doubts: Some features may have many zeros for customers with short history. Log-transform recommended before clustering.

status_messages.what_was_done: Scored 290 features (PCA+AE). Removed 176 via VIF gate. Claude selected 42 from 114 survivors.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not apply p-value gate (sufficient features remain after VIF gate).
status_messages.doubts: 5 high-correlation pairs still present after VIF gate — Claude was asked to avoid both in each pair.

status_messages.what_was_done: Used KMeans with k=15 (auto-selected via silhouette). Ran deepening loop → 15 leaf clusters. Silhouette=0.1461 (weak but usable).
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not try DBSCAN or GMM (not in current skill set).
status_messages.doubts: Silhouette improved 0.111→0.146 across k range — marginal gain.
issues: Silhouette=0.146 < 0.15 — clusters overlap; interpretability may be low. Consider reviewing feature selection or k.

status_messages.what_was_done: Named 15 clusters using Claude (tone='easy'). Clarity Gate FAILED. Avg confidence=1.0/10.
status_messages.what_was_not_done: Did not validate that description text references specific numbers (only checked confidence and name uniqueness).
status_messages.doubts: Low-confidence clusters: C0(conf=1), C1(conf=1), C2(conf=1), C3(conf=1), C4(conf=1), C5(conf=1), C6(conf=1), C7(conf=1), C8(conf=1), C9(conf=1), C10(conf=1), C11(conf=1), C12(conf=1), C13(conf=1), C14(conf=1)
issues: Avg LLM confidence 1.0 < 6.0
